title: 1.1: States that the OSC 133 escape sequences (A, B, C, D) pass through in the captured output unchanged.
1.2: States the total byte length of the captured output containing OSC 133 sequences for exit code 42 as numeric values like 77 bytes.
1.3: States the byte offset where the D;42 marker appears in the captured output as numeric value like 71.
1.4: States the byte length across the exit codes (0, 1, 127) as numeric values such as 76, 78 etc.
1.5: States that the byte offset position for the exit code marker across all tested exit codes (0, 1, 127) as numeric value like 71.
1.6: States the presence of "D;99" in the captured output, when test was run with exit code 99, proving that the value 99 was processed and passed through the entire code path.
1.7: States the recorded exit status value when sending OSC 133;D;not_a_number as 0.
1.8: States the recorded exit status value when sending OSC 133;D; with empty exit code as 0.
